title: Device API & wiring
lead: This page documents how devices integrate with Flovers. Devices send sensor readings to the ingest endpoint and can read the latest device feed from the feed endpoint, for example to trigger actions such as launching a water pump. You’ll also find the payload format, a generated Arduino firmware example for sending readings, and a simple ESP32/Arduino control example for reading the device feed and activating watering.

endpoints.title: API endpoints
endpoints.note: Devices send telemetry to the ingest endpoint and can read the latest device feed from the feed endpoint.

ingest.title: POST ingest documentation
ingest.desc: Send one reading sample as JSON. The backend authenticates the device using device_key and secret, then stores the provided metrics under that device.
ingest.arduinoDesc: The generated firmware connects to Wi-Fi, synchronizes time with NTP, reads enabled sensors, and posts JSON to the ingest endpoint on a fixed interval.
ingest.arduinoNote: Use the generated firmware as provided for the device. Before uploading, set Wi-Fi credentials and verify sensor wiring, pin assignments, and soil calibration values.

sensors.moisture: Capacitive soil moisture sensor — analog read with wet/dry calibration
sensors.bme280: BME280 — temperature & humidity over I2C (pressure optional)

feed.title: GET feed documentation
feed.desc: Read the latest device feed from the backend. Devices can use this endpoint to receive current state or actions, for example launching a water pump.
feed.controlDesc: This example shows how an ESP32 can call the feed endpoint, parse the latest response, compare soil moisture against a defined threshold, and activate a relay-controlled water pump for a configurable duration. After watering or checking conditions, the device waits for a configurable interval before checking again.
feed.controlCodeLabel: ESP32 / Arduino example — GET feed and trigger pump
feed.controlNote: Adjust relay logic, pump runtime, moisture threshold, and polling delay for your hardware. Many relay modules are active LOW, so verify whether LOW or HIGH turns your relay on.

wiring.title: Wiring diagrams
wiring.desc: Below is a sample device wiring schema for sending POST requests to the Flovers backend. Click the image to open it in full screen.
wiring.ariaPrimary: Sample device wiring schema for POST requests
